beats.narration_text: Vanakkam. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of bloom filter: exponential precision for linear space. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of bloom filter: exponential precision for linear space
output: ✓ Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space

beats.narration_text: Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. ε ≈ (1/2)^(b·ln 2)  |  b = bits per inserted object  |  False positives shrink exponentially as b grows linearly. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: bSlider. Verify: ε ≈ (1/2)^(b·ln 2)  |  b = bits per inserted object  |  False positives shrink exponentially as b grows linearly. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: bSlider. Verify: ε

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: bloom filter: exponential precision for linear space. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 4 to 32. Watch the physics respond in real time.

beats.narration_text: ε ≈ (1/2)^(b·ln 2)  |  b = bits per inserted object  |  False positives shrink exponentially as b grows linearly. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of bloom filter: exponential precision for linear space. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Bloom Filter: Exponential Precision for Linear Space simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
